section: P2.1 Version The Universal Concept Contract
verbatim: Keep one corpus-lexicon system; do not create a parallel concept store.
action: Enforce at every S5/S10 lexicon-touching merge: all new concept fields land inside Mongo corpus_lexicon (corpus_lexicon.v3 -> v4 bump) and its existing Qdrant entity_lexicon projection; reject any change adding a second concept collection. Verify per merge via Mongo collection listing + grep for new concept/lexicon collection names outside corpus_lexicon.
where: backend/services/ingestion/corpus_lexicon.py; Mongo corpus_lexicon; Qdrant entity_lexicon points
why: One authoritative store keeps concept evidence joinable, rebuildable, and epoch-invalidatable for retrieval.
feasibility_note: corpus_lexicon.v3 already IS the single store (LEXICON_SCHEMA_VERSION at corpus_lexicon.py:37). Terminology: registry_latent flags SPLIT REQUIRED — 'universal concept contract' (P2.1) = corpus_lexicon entry schema, NOT the Ghost A summary capture contract (T-HOOK-2 seam); registry_alias warns S5's versioned alias registry must FOLD corpus_lexicon aliases + latent_concepts[].aliases, not become a third store; say 'corpus_lexicon' for the store, never bare 'vocabulary' (that is the query-time resolution stage).
required_receipt: Post-merge probe: db.getCollectionNames() shows exactly one concept store (corpus_lexicon) + its entity_lexicon Qdrant projection; grep output attached to the merge showing no parallel concept collection introduced.

section: P2.1 Version The Universal Concept Contract
verbatim: Define canonical name, aliases, abbreviations, definitions, contextual usages, applications, components, source IDs, entity IDs/types, and evidence.
action: Diff the corpus_lexicon.v3 entry schema field-by-field against this ten-item list; produce a present/missing table (aliases, alias_evidence, definitions, acronym pairs, applications, components, factual relations, source provenance already exist in v3) and version only the genuine gaps into corpus_lexicon.v4 with a typed model.
who: backend service (corpus_lexicon builder) + junior-agent schema audit
where: backend/services/ingestion/corpus_lexicon.py (mine_entity_text_evidence, mine_acronym_pairs, accumulator at ~L720-1270); Mongo corpus_lexicon
why: Complete identity+evidence fields are what let lay-language vocabulary nominate the right documents.
feasibility_note: Largely present in v3: aliases/aliases_normalized/alias_evidence (corpus_lexicon.py:720-724,978-992), definitions via explicit_definition_pattern, Schwartz-Hearst acronyms, structural contexts, applications/components/factual_relations, source-level provenance (LEXICON_SOURCE_VERSION). Terminology: entity IDs MUST use neo4j_writer.entity_id_from_name (hyphen convention — the single source of truth per registry_alias); 'lexicon entry' = one corpus_lexicon document keyed lexicon_id.
required_receipt: Schema-diff artifact mapping each contract field -> present-in-v3 | added-in-v4, plus one sampled entry per PoC corpus passing typed-model validation (P0.8 writer-boundary style, non-zero exit on violation).

section: P2.1 Version The Universal Concept Contract
verbatim: Add field-level provenance and validation status.
action: Generalize the existing alias_evidence pattern (per-item method + evidence + bounded snippet) to every field family in the entry, and add a per-field validation_status enum; extend the P0.8 validators to assert it on write.
where: backend/services/ingestion/corpus_lexicon.py (alias_evidence accumulator ~L984-992 as template); Mongo corpus_lexicon; P0.8 typed-model validators
why: Field-level provenance is what makes the four provenance classes auditable and lets gates trust or exclude a field.
feasibility_note: The pattern already exists for aliases (method + evidence per item); this extends it, not invents it. Terminology: registry_extraction — 'provenance' is an umbrella; name the concrete mechanism per field (extractor/schema_version, summary_model, lexicon source-ID lists, relation source_predicate) rather than a bare 'provenance' flag.
required_receipt: One rebuilt lexicon entry (UGO canary) showing method+source+validation_status on every populated field family; P0.8 validator run rejecting a synthetic entry with an unprovenanced field.

section: P2.1 Version The Universal Concept Contract
verbatim: Separate source-extracted facts from generated user-language material.
action: Implement the split as a mandatory provenance_class on every field/sub-item (rides the same v4 schema touch as L843's four classes); assert at the writer boundary that no generated-class material can appear in an extracted-class field.
where: backend/services/ingestion/corpus_lexicon.py; Mongo corpus_lexicon; P0.8 writer-boundary validator
why: Generated user-language may nominate but must never masquerade as source evidence in a required answer lane.
feasibility_note: Same seam as L843 — do them in one schema pass (Standing Rule 4, one migration per seam). Today v3 is entirely deterministic/extracted, so the split is cheap now and expensive after S10's generated methods exist. Terminology: 'generated' here = the P2.1 provenance class, distinct from 'attested' (validated real user phrasing).
required_receipt: Writer-boundary test: upsert of a lexicon entry mixing generated text into an extracted field fails with non-zero exit; schema doc showing the class field is required.

section: P2.1 Version The Universal Concept Contract
verbatim: Add salience counters: evidence count, document count, graph support, and resolver hit count.
action: Compute evidence_count, document_count, and graph_support (count of typed factual_relations projections) deterministically during the S5 lexicon rematerialization; initialize resolver_hit_count=0 and wire its only increment path to S10's attested-loop gate (P2V:L912), never to raw query traffic.
who: backend service (corpus_lexicon builder); increments via retriever vocabulary resolution
where: backend/services/ingestion/corpus_lexicon.py; backend/services/retriever/vocabulary.py; Mongo corpus_lexicon
why: Salience feeds specificity admission and card seed strength — the difference between a real concept and lexicon noise.
feasibility_note: Three of four counters are pure aggregations over data that exists post-S4/S5 (plan S5 row: rollup 'feeds P2.1 salience/DF fields'). resolver_hit_count is deferred-by-design: its deterministic increment rule is defined at P2.2's attested loop (checklist L912), so it ships dark-but-named with its consumer anchored (Standing Rule 2 satisfied by the anchor). Terminology: 'graph support' = count over the typed factual relations projection (registry_extraction), not associated_concepts co-occurrence.
required_receipt: Post-S5 Mongo probe on the PoC pair: counters present on 100% of entries, spot-checked against manual counts for 5 concepts; resolver_hit_count uniformly 0 until S10.

section: P2.1 Version The Universal Concept Contract
verbatim: Add chunk-derived `associated_concepts` using parent/chunk co-occurrence, PMI or log-likelihood weight, support count, and the intersecting evidence parent/chunk IDs. Keep these associations distinct from typed factual graph relations.
action: Build associated_concepts as a derived-class field during S5 rematerialization, computed only through L828's bounded inverted index; store weight metric name+value, support count, and intersecting parent/chunk IDs; keep it in a separate field from factual_relations and never project it into Neo4j typed edges.
who: backend service (corpus_lexicon builder / S5 rollup script)
where: backend/services/ingestion/corpus_lexicon.py; Mongo corpus_lexicon; input = parent_chunks concept fields (latent_concepts, concept_tags, key_terms)
why: Co-occurrence neighborhoods give the librarian cheap deterministic relatedness without polluting the typed factual graph.
feasibility_note: SEQUENCING GUARD: the PoC pair has ZERO parents with latent_concepts today (PLAN_CRITIQUE sec.2, V) — co-occurrence input only exists after S4's capture pass; running this before S4 violates Standing Rule 1 (capture-before-rebuild). Terminology: keep 'associated_concepts' (derived) strictly apart from 'typed factual relations' (RelationItem -> ghost_b_extractions -> Neo4j, registry_extraction); the checklist itself mandates the distinction.
required_receipt: Post-S5 sample of 10 entries on mark: associated_concepts populated with weight+support+intersecting IDs that re-verify against a direct Mongo co-occurrence count; Neo4j probe showing zero edges created from this field.

section: P2.1 Version The Universal Concept Contract
verbatim: Compute co-occurrence through a bounded parent/chunk-to-concept inverted index with minimum support, significance, specificity, and per-concept neighbor caps; never perform an unbounded all-concept pairwise scan or let one boilerplate parent create a combinatorial association hub.
action: Implement the co-occurrence pass as parent->concept inverted index with configured min_support, significance threshold, specificity filter, and per-concept neighbor cap; add a per-parent concept-count ceiling so one boilerplate parent cannot fan out; assert runtime and max-neighbor bounds in the build log.
where: S5 rollup script + backend/services/ingestion/corpus_lexicon.py; reads parent_chunks; writes corpus_lexicon.associated_concepts
why: Bounded computation is the only thing standing between associated_concepts and a combinatorial hub that poisons expansion.
feasibility_note: The hub failure mode is empirically proven in this system: the broad-alias facet family stamps 71-100% of every corpus (agency_preservation on 89,088 parents, PLAN_CRITIQUE sec.2 V) and is DF-worthless. Scale is manageable — v2 (130,503 parents) is FROZEN for heavy ops; the pass runs on the PoC pair (~11,237 parents) + UGO. This claim is both a build item and a standing computational constraint on any future re-run.
required_receipt: Index build log artifact: total pairs evaluated << all-pairs bound, max neighbors per concept <= cap, per-parent ceiling hits counted, wall-clock recorded; re-run produces identical output (deterministic).

section: P2.1 Version The Universal Concept Contract
verbatim: Add source-backed `usage_frames` by harvesting KWIC sentences around canonical/alias mentions and extracting dependency frames such as `use X to`, `X prevents`, and `apply X when`; retain sentence boundaries, source IDs, parser/pattern version, and confidence.
action: Build a deterministic KWIC + pattern-frame harvesting lane (start pattern-based, not full dependency parsing) over parent text for canonical/alias mentions; store frames as derived-class lexicon material with sentence boundaries, source IDs, parser_or_pattern_version, and confidence; measure runtime on UGO before running the pair.
who: backend service (corpus_lexicon builder, new harvesting module)
where: backend/services/ingestion/corpus_lexicon.py (alongside mine_entity_text_evidence); Mongo corpus_lexicon.usage_frames; reads parent_chunks text
why: Usage frames are the deterministic feedstock for P2.2's utility and templated-question representation points.
feasibility_note: No parser or frame-extraction code exists in the repo (grep: zero hits for usage_frame). A dependency parser is a new heavyweight dependency; the pattern-lane subset (`use X to`, `X prevents`, `apply X when`) is deterministic regex-over-sentences and matches the existing mine_entity_text_evidence style — recommend shipping the pattern lane first and treating full dependency frames as a later versioned parser upgrade (the contract already requires parser/pattern version, so the split is legal). Cost on 11k PoC parents unmeasured — canary on UGO (203 parents) first.
required_receipt: UGO canary run: usage_frames present with all five provenance sub-fields, 20-frame manual precision spot-check >= agreed threshold set in advance (hypothesis-promotion rule), runtime recorded; then pair run log.

section: P2.1 Version The Universal Concept Contract
verbatim: Add a `semantic_profile` that aggregates supporting-parent `semantic_chunk_type` distributions and validated mechanism IDs so the card records whether the corpus treats the concept as a definition, procedure, principle, warning, example, or another evidence shape.
action: Build semantic_profile as a pure aggregation projection inside corpus_lexicon: for each entry, histogram supporting-parent semantic_chunk_type values and attach validated mechanism IDs; do NOT create a new extraction lane.
who: backend service (corpus_lexicon builder / S5 rollup script)
where: backend/services/ingestion/corpus_lexicon.py; reads parent_chunks.semantic_chunk_type + mechanisms; writes corpus_lexicon.semantic_profile
why: Knowing whether the corpus treats a concept as definition vs procedure vs warning lets cards and answer-shape routing pick the right evidence shape.
feasibility_note: registry_latent names this row exactly: 'semantic_profile (to-be-built P2.1 lexicon/card field)' and its duplicate_implementation_risk warns to build it as a projection over EXISTING parent_chunks.semantic_chunk_type, not a new extraction lane. Inputs exist today (semantic_chunk_type is stored per parent; mechanisms in CONCEPT_SOURCE_FIELDS). Pure deterministic aggregation — cheap, idempotent.
required_receipt: Post-S5 probe: semantic_profile on eligible pair entries whose distribution sums match a direct parent_chunks aggregation query for 5 sampled concepts.

section: P2.1 Version The Universal Concept Contract
verbatim: Store `df`, `corpus_n`, specificity/IDF, support distribution, and hub status per entry. Generic/high-degree concepts may retain evidence but cannot gain expansion or representation rights without passing specificity gates.
action: Compute per-entry df, corpus_n, IDF/specificity, support distribution, and hub_status during S5 rematerialization; wire the specificity gate into grounded vocabulary expansion (deny expansion rights) and into L849's representation-point admission (deny points); evidence retention unaffected.
who: backend service (corpus_lexicon builder) + retriever vocabulary resolution (gate consumer)
where: backend/services/ingestion/corpus_lexicon.py; backend/services/retriever/vocabulary.py (expansion lane); Mongo corpus_lexicon
why: Specificity is the concept-side defense against the exact hub pathology already measured on facets.
feasibility_note: Concept-side twin of the S7 facet DF rule — plan S7 row explicitly anchors 'P2.1 specificity gates'. The facet analogue has verified evidence (broad-alias family 71-100% DF, PLAN_CRITIQUE sec.2 V) but the concept-side threshold is a NEW calibration: set numeric thresholds in advance and calibrate on the pair (hypothesis-promotion rule). Terminology: registry_facet's 'parent_df' is the FACET DF — the lexicon df here is a distinct per-concept computation; do not reuse the facet symbol.
required_receipt: Post-S5 probe: fields present on all entries; top-10 highest-DF concepts listed with hub_status=true; live vocabulary-resolution trace showing a hub concept denied expansion while its evidence remains retrievable.

section: P2.1 Version The Universal Concept Contract
verbatim: Version four provenance classes independently: `extracted` (provider or deterministic source extraction), `derived` (joins, frames, templates), `attested` (validated real user phrasing), and `generated` (optional LLM).
action: Add a provenance_class enum {extracted, derived, attested, generated} with an independent version stamp per class (e.g. extracted.v1, derived.v1) to the corpus_lexicon.v4 schema; rides the same S5 schema pass as L820/L821; attested and generated stay empty until S10 but the classes and their version fields ship now.
where: backend/services/ingestion/corpus_lexicon.py; Mongo corpus_lexicon; P0.8 validators
why: Independent class versions let one class be rebuilt or invalidated without touching the others' epochs.
feasibility_note: registry_extraction lists 'provenance classes (planned)' under the provenance umbrella — named, unbuilt, no collision. All current v3 content is extracted/derived, so classification of existing material is deterministic. 'attested' must mean ONLY validated real user phrasing per L912's gates — never planner output; 'generated' is Phase-4-gated (L929).
required_receipt: Schema artifact showing the four classes with independent version fields; migration log classifying 100% of existing v3 material as extracted or derived with zero attested/generated rows pre-S10.

section: P2.1 Version The Universal Concept Contract
verbatim: Keep Mongo `corpus_lexicon` authoritative and make every Qdrant concept point an idempotent, fully rebuildable projection with projection/artifact epoch and deterministic point identity.
action: Verify the existing deterministic idempotent Qdrant lexicon projection (corpus_lexicon.py:2786 docstring: retrying a cursor range overwrites the same deterministic Qdrant IDs) and extend point payloads with an explicit projection/artifact epoch stamp; prove rebuild-twice idempotency and epoch-driven cache invalidation on UGO.
where: backend/services/ingestion/corpus_lexicon.py (~L2780-2800, finalize_corpus_lexicon_index, _bump_vocabulary_cache_epoch:3373); backend/services/storage/qdrant_writer.py (upsert_lexicon_entries); Qdrant entity_lexicon points
why: Rebuildable projections are what make every later representation method disposable without lexicon rebuilds.
feasibility_note: MOSTLY IN CODE: deterministic idempotent point IDs and per-corpus vocabulary cache epoch bumps already exist (probed today); the gap is the explicit projection/artifact epoch field ON the point payload. The epoch-invalidation half depends on S0's live verification of the P1.7 cache (PLAN_CRITIQUE S0 row: 'IN CODE awaiting live verify'). Mongo-authoritative is already the design (corpus_lexicon.py module docstring).
required_receipt: Rebuild-twice probe on UGO: identical point IDs both runs, artifact epoch incremented, and a cached vocabulary resolution demonstrably invalidated after the epoch bump (S0 cache receipt extended).

section: P2.1 Version The Universal Concept Contract
verbatim: Require `retrieval_eligible=true`, valid source ownership, evidence bindings, and specificity admission before any entry emits query-facing representation points.
action: Extend the existing retrieval_eligible gate (already enforced at corpus_lexicon.py:2015 and vocabulary.py:486/673/1312/2241) into a four-condition admission function (eligible + source ownership + evidence bindings + specificity from L840) and make it the sole entry point for S10 representation-point emission.
who: backend service (corpus_lexicon admission function; consumed by S10 projector)
where: backend/services/ingestion/corpus_lexicon.py; backend/services/retriever/vocabulary.py; future P2.2 point projector
why: Admission-before-emission is the wall that keeps junk and hub concepts out of the query-facing vector space.
feasibility_note: PARTIALLY IN CODE: retrieval_eligible exists and is respected at four resolver call sites (probed today). Evidence-binding and specificity conditions depend on L840/S5 fields. Terminology: 'representation points' = the P2.2 multi-method Qdrant points (to-be-built), distinct from today's entity_lexicon identity points — the admission function must gate the NEW kind without breaking the existing one.
required_receipt: Unit + integration test: an entry failing any one of the four conditions emits zero representation points while its entity_lexicon identity point and evidence remain; test is asserting with non-zero exit.

section: P2.1 Version The Universal Concept Contract
verbatim: Use globally stable concept identity while retaining `corpus_id` and source ownership.
action: Define global concept identity as the normalize_identity canonical_key (already the cross-system keyspace) while keeping lexicon_id + corpus_id per-corpus; document the join rule (global canonical_key groups per-corpus entries; ownership never merges) and add it to the schema doc.
who: backend service (corpus_lexicon) — design codification, not new computation
where: backend/services/ingestion/corpus_lexicon.py (normalize_identity:192); Mongo corpus_lexicon (lexicon_id, corpus_id)
why: Cross-corpus merge (L943) and librarian-card overlap need one identity spine without corpus boundary leaks.
feasibility_note: The substrate exists: registry_alias — canonical_key = normalize_identity keyspace (aliased value_key in librarian cards), and the owner architecture names an 'identity spine'. The only design tension is collision risk across corpora for same-surface different-meaning concepts; corpus_id retention (this claim's second half) is the mitigation. No owner decision needed — the contract text already decides the shape.
required_receipt: Cross-corpus probe: the same concept surface in mark and ecom yields one canonical_key with two corpus-owned entries; retrieval scoped to one corpus never returns the other's entry.

section: P2.1 Version The Universal Concept Contract
verbatim: Define `librarian_card.v0` fields and deterministic seed contracts: central_subjects; mechanisms_taught; capabilities_developed; problems_addressed (only explicit definitional/problem-shaped evidence); transferable_principles; risks_or_likely_misuse (empty in v0 unless explicit warning evidence); counterbalancing_concepts (empty in v0 unless typed/evidence-backed); evidence_spans.
action: Audit card_builder.py field-by-field against these eight seed contracts (schema exists; 673 cards built 2026-07-13), fix any seed-derivation gaps, then rebuild pair cards ONCE at S8 after S4 latent capture + S5 rollup + S7 facet decision land (Standing Rule 1 — the 2026-07-13 build ran before its feed existed, critique C2).
where: backend/services/librarian/card_builder.py; Mongo librarian_cards; inputs: corpus_lexicon, parent_chunks.latent_concepts, doc_profile.concepts
why: The card is the librarian's routing object; wrong or empty seeds misroute every downstream shelf decision.
feasibility_note: SCHEMA LARGELY IN CODE, DATA KNOWN-BAD: card_builder.py exists and consumes latent_concepts (PLAN_CRITIQUE V), central_subjects and counterbalancing_concepts exist per registry_librarian/latent — but pair cards were built with 0% latent input and ~0 bibliographic fields (critique C2), so current cards structurally miss their contract. Terminology: librarian_card.v0 is canonical; central_subjects is renamed 'subjects' in slim payloads; 'counterbalance' is ALSO a shelf role — the card field is counterbalancing_concepts (keep them apart); candidate_latent_subjects already implements the latent->card path (do not add a second latent seed — registry_latent duplicate risk).
required_receipt: Conformance table: 8 fields x (seed contract | card_builder derivation | match/gap); post-S8 rebuilt pair cards with latent-fed fields non-empty where evidence exists and v0-empty fields (risks, counterbalancing) empty absent typed evidence.

section: P2.1 Version The Universal Concept Contract
verbatim: Record `field -> method -> source IDs -> confidence` for every populated card field.
action: Add a per-field provenance block (method, source IDs, confidence) to librarian_card.v0 output in card_builder.py; validate at write that every populated field carries it; rides the S8 pair card rebuild.
where: backend/services/librarian/card_builder.py; Mongo librarian_cards
why: Per-field card provenance is what lets an LLM explain — but never alter — a seated selection.
feasibility_note: Registry_extraction already reserves 'per-field provenance (cards)' under the provenance umbrella. Cheap deterministic projection work; cards are rebuildable (critique C2 calls them 'cheap deterministic projections'). Must ride S8's single rebuild — a separate provenance-only rebuild before S8 would violate the rebuild-once constraint.
required_receipt: Post-S8 probe: 100% of populated fields across pair cards carry method+source IDs+confidence; one sampled field's source IDs re-resolve to real parent/chunk/tree rows.

section: P2.1 Version The Universal Concept Contract
verbatim: Penalize generic/high-degree principle hubs using specificity/IDF and support-distribution checks before they can justify transfer.
action: Consume L840's specificity/IDF + support-distribution fields inside card_builder's transferable_principles seeding: a concept below the specificity threshold or with hub_status=true cannot seed transferable_principles regardless of frequency; log each rejection.
who: backend service (card_builder.py, consuming S5 lexicon fields)
where: backend/services/librarian/card_builder.py; corpus_lexicon (df/idf/hub_status inputs)
why: Without a hub penalty, 'take action' outranks a real transferable principle in every cross-domain card.
feasibility_note: Depends entirely on S5's L840 substrate (compute) — this is the card-side consumer, correctly slotted at S8's rebuild. The facet twin (S7 broad-alias family, verified 71-100% DF) proves the pathology class in this data. Threshold numbers must be preregistered before the S8 rebuild (hypothesis-promotion rule; registry_eval notes 'preregistered thresholds' exist nowhere yet).
required_receipt: S8 rebuild log listing hub-rejected principle candidates with their IDF/support numbers; spot-check that a known-generic concept (top-DF list) appears in no card's transferable_principles.

section: P2.1 Version The Universal Concept Contract
verbatim: Publish slim Tier-0 card payloads only after pointer-integrity validation.
action: Add a pointer-integrity pass before the Tier-0 projection publish: every evidence_spans parent/chunk/tree ID and every source ID on the slim payload must resolve against Mongo; publication aborts (non-zero exit) on any dangling pointer; run as part of the S8 'Mongo + slim Tier-0 projection' step.
who: script (projection publisher with asserting validation) + backend tier0 projection reconcile
where: backend/services/librarian/card_builder.py -> Qdrant polymath_doc_summaries / tier0_routing_card projection; Mongo librarian_cards
why: A slim routing payload with dead pointers seats documents on evidence that cannot be hydrated or cited.
feasibility_note: Terminology: 'slim Tier-0 card payloads' (checklist:891/895) = the tier0_routing_card projection (registry_retrieval canonical; aka doc_summaries collection); central_subjects appears as 'subjects' in this slim form. The reconciliation family already includes 'tier0 projection reconcile' (registry_readiness) — extend that mechanism rather than adding a parallel validator.
required_receipt: S8 publish log: pointer-integrity pass result 0 dangling / N checked, publication gated on it; negative test with a synthetic dangling ID aborts publish.

section: P2.2 Build Two-Sided Multi-Point Concept Representations
verbatim: The concept record is a two-sided join: query-shaped representations nominate the concept, while source document/parent/chunk bindings identify where its evidence lives. Vocabulary nominates; hydrated chunks still testify.
action: Accept as the adopted P2.2 design frame; re-verify at S10 review that every implemented representation method conforms (each point carries evidence bindings; no point ever substitutes for hydrated chunk evidence).
who: owner (already decided) / junior-agent conformance check at S10
where: docs/RAPTOR_RAG_IMPLEMENTATION_CHECKLIST.md P2.2 preamble; future point projector + retriever
why: The nominate-vs-testify split is the load-bearing rule that keeps vocabulary from ever replacing evidence.
feasibility_note: This is design prose, not work; it restates two non-negotiable invariants (deterministic evidence-backed seating; final evidence retains provenance) and matches the owner retrieval architecture (two-lane anchoring, hydration waterfall). No conflict with any registry; it is the same rule L940 operationalizes.
required_receipt: None to close (kind=decision, ledger na); S10 conformance note citing this preamble against the shipped point schema.

section: P2.2 Build Two-Sided Multi-Point Concept Representations
verbatim: Complete the P1.1 held-out baseline and contamination firewall.
action: Verify the P1.1 baseline artifact (the held-out 56, 2026-07-13 eval baseline) exists and is the S8/S10 comparison anchor; build the remaining half — the AUTOMATED contamination eval-run check — which the plan explicitly lands 'with the first representation store' at S10.
where: backend/services/eval_firewall.py; run_heldout_eval harness; docs/baselines eval artifacts
why: Without the firewall, attested harvesting can memorize the eval questions and every recall gain becomes fiction.
feasibility_note: PARTIALLY DONE: P1.1 baseline is [x] per PLAN_CRITIQUE S10 row ('Prereqs now real: P1.1 baseline [x]'); eval_firewall.py exists and already handles attested/query_hash (code probe today). The open remainder per checklist status table (P1.1: 3 open) = librarian rubric, automated contamination eval-run check, standing before/after gate. Terminology: registry_eval — 'eval_firewall' is the primitive; 'automated contamination eval-run check' is the named unbuilt half; the suite is 'the held-out 56'.
required_receipt: Eval-run preflight output asserting 0 held-out hashes present in any attested/representation store, wired to non-zero exit, executed automatically at the start of every eval run (receipt = one green run log + one red synthetic-contamination run log).

section: P2.2 Build Two-Sided Multi-Point Concept Representations
verbatim: Complete P1.7 batching, vector reuse, resolver cache, epoch invalidation, and exact/lexical-only latency baseline.
action: Execute S0: merge/deploy and LIVE-VERIFY the P1.7 vocabulary cache + epoch invalidation (both IN CODE awaiting live verify per PLAN_CRITIQUE S0 row); then capture the exact/lexical-only latency baseline; the remaining P1.7 boxes (gather-before-fanout, vector reuse, per-corpus batching, exact-lookup separation) close per the checklist before S10 may start.
who: backend service (retriever vocabulary + embedder) + junior-agent live probe
where: backend/services/retriever/vocabulary.py (P1.7 cache comment :1697); backend/services/retriever/vocabulary_cache.py (bump_corpus_epoch); backend/services/embedder.py (_QueryEmbedBatcher :693, query cache :816-881)
why: P2.2 multiplies vocabulary query volume; without batching+cache its latency cost is unbounded and unmeasurable.
feasibility_note: Substantial code exists NOW: query-embed cache with keyed invalidation (embedder.py:816-881), per-corpus epoch bumps at the lexicon seam (corpus_lexicon.py:3373-3391), P1.7 cache keyed by query/lanes/corpus-set/knobs+epoch (vocabulary.py:1697 comment). Checklist status: P1.7 = 0 [x], 2 IN CODE, 8 open — 'complete' here means live-verified + baselined, not written. This is the S0 dark-code payoff (critique C3).
required_receipt: S0 live-verify artifact: cache hit/miss counters over a repeated-query probe, an epoch bump demonstrably invalidating the cache, and a recorded exact/lexical-only latency baseline (p50/p95) filed with docs/baselines.

section: P2.2 Build Two-Sided Multi-Point Concept Representations
verbatim: Select only P2.1 entries that pass retrieval eligibility, ownership, evidence, salience, and specificity admission.
action: Make the S10 point projector consume L849's admission function as its ONLY input iterator (no direct corpus_lexicon scans); log admitted/rejected counts per corpus per run.
where: future P2.2 projector module; backend/services/ingestion/corpus_lexicon.py admission function; Mongo corpus_lexicon
why: Selection discipline at the projector is what makes acceptance L955 (hubs emit no points) structurally true.
feasibility_note: Straightforward once S5 lands L840/L849; blocked until then (admission fields do not exist yet — grep today: no df/salience/hub fields in corpus_lexicon.py). Duplicate-gate risk: this must be the SAME admission function as L849, not a re-implementation in the projector.
required_receipt: Projector run log on mark: admitted N / rejected M with per-condition rejection counts; zero points found in Qdrant for any entry with retrieval_eligible=false or hub_status=true.

section: P2.2 Build Two-Sided Multi-Point Concept Representations
verbatim: Pilot on one small strict-ready corpus with a configured maximum number of representation points per concept before projecting large corpora.
action: Run the S10 pilot on markbuildsbrands_transcripts (the plan's designated pilot: 'pilot on mark with per-concept point caps') with a configured max-points-per-concept; before calling it 'strict-ready', grade mark through S6's readiness split since the strict-ready definition (checklist:1252-1268) is 0/10 built.
who: script (pilot projection run) + backend readiness service
where: P2.2 projector; markbuildsbrands_transcripts (1,015 parents); services/ingestion/readiness.py (S6 split)
why: A capped small-corpus pilot bounds the blast radius of the first query-facing concept vectors.
feasibility_note: Corpus choice is already decided (PLAN_CRITIQUE S10 row names mark) — not an owner decision. GAP: 'strict-ready' is a defined-but-unbuilt gate (registry_readiness: strict_ready = checklist:1252-1268; status table: 0 [x], 10 open, consumed by S6) — the pilot precondition cannot be literally certified until S6 ships; the plan's S-order (S6 before S10) already resolves this, so the claim is feasible as sequenced, infeasible if S10 jumps the queue.
required_receipt: Pilot manifest: corpus=mark, per-concept cap value, S6 readiness grade for mark attached; Qdrant count probe showing max points per canonical_key <= cap.

section: P2.2 Build Two-Sided Multi-Point Concept Representations
verbatim: Emit separate points for `name_alias`, `definition`, `utility`, and `templated_question_problem` representation methods instead of forcing every query shape into one compromise gloss vector.
action: Implement the four representation_method emitters in the S10 projector, each reading only its indexed source field (aliases -> name_alias; definitions -> definition; utility_gloss -> utility; usage_frames/templates -> templated_question_problem); embed and upsert per-method points tagged with representation_method.
where: P2.2 projector; corpus_lexicon fields (aliases, definitions, utility_gloss, usage_frames); Qdrant representation points
why: Method-separated points are the whole lay-language recall bet — one gloss vector cannot match four query shapes.
feasibility_note: Terminology receipt: registry_extraction maps the 'utility' method to the EXISTING corpus_lexicon utility_gloss field ('planned embedded projection of the same material') — reuse it, do not synthesize new utility text. name_alias/definition sources exist in v3 today; templated_question_problem depends on S5 usage_frames (L832, flagged risky) — that method can lag the other three without blocking the pilot.
required_receipt: Qdrant probe on mark pilot: points exist for each of the four representation_method values (or three + documented usage_frames deferral), each point's text traceable to its named source field.

section: P2.2 Build Two-Sided Multi-Point Concept Representations
verbatim: Build deterministic questions/problems only from indexed fields and usage frames, such as `What is X?`, `How do you <usage frame>?`, and `How to <application context>?`; every point must remain span/source backed.
action: Implement template expansion strictly over indexed lexicon fields (canonical name, usage_frames, application contexts) with a versioned template set; carry the source span IDs of the feeding field onto every generated point; forbid free-text synthesis in this lane.
where: P2.2 projector; corpus_lexicon (usage_frames, applications); Qdrant templated_question_problem points
why: Deterministic templates buy question-shaped recall without LLM cost or drift — the measured-lift bar for Phase 4.
feasibility_note: Hard dependency on L832 usage_frames (S5, graded risky): 'How do you <usage frame>?' has no input until frames exist. 'What is X?' needs only canonical name and can ship immediately. Provenance class of these points = derived (registry: templates are derived, NOT generated) — mislabeling them generated would wrongly subject them to Phase-4 gating; mislabeling extracted would overstate evidence.
required_receipt: Sample of 20 templated points on mark: each carries template version + feeding field + span/source IDs that resolve; zero points whose text is not a deterministic function of indexed fields.

section: P2.2 Build Two-Sided Multi-Point Concept Representations
verbatim: Store `canonical_key`, `lexicon_id`, `corpus_id`, `representation_method`, provenance class, evidence IDs, artifact epoch, validation status, and `exploratory` on every point.
action: Define the representation-point payload schema with these nine fields required, validate at upsert (extend qdrant_writer with a typed payload check), and add Qdrant payload indexes for canonical_key, representation_method, and corpus_id.
where: backend/services/storage/qdrant_writer.py; Qdrant representation points + payload indexes
why: These payload fields are what make grouping (L901), gating (L904), and rebuild-by-method (L899) possible at query time.
feasibility_note: Terminology: canonical_key = the normalize_identity keyspace (registry_alias; aliased value_key in cards) — use that exact function, echoing the entity-id lesson (one canonical id function, no parallel slugs). Payload-index additions must ride P1.9's filter-index audit discipline (checklist P1.9 open items) so the new indexes are counted, not ad-hoc.
required_receipt: Upsert validation test rejecting a point missing any required field; Qdrant collection info showing the three payload indexes; sampled point displaying all nine fields.

section: P2.2 Build Two-Sided Multi-Point Concept Representations
verbatim: Use deterministic point IDs so methods can be rebuilt, superseded, or removed independently without rebuilding the authoritative lexicon.
action: Derive point IDs deterministically from (lexicon_id, representation_method, item ordinal, artifact epoch policy) — mirroring the existing lexicon projection's idempotent-ID pattern (corpus_lexicon.py:2786) — and prove per-method rebuild/delete leaves other methods' points and Mongo untouched.
where: P2.2 projector; Qdrant representation points; pattern source: backend/services/ingestion/corpus_lexicon.py:2780-2800
why: Method-independent rebuild is what makes each representation method a disposable experiment instead of a migration.
feasibility_note: The exact pattern is already proven in this codebase — the entity_lexicon projection retries overwrite the same deterministic Qdrant IDs (probed today). Design note: decide whether epoch is IN the ID (supersede-by-new-id + delete-old) or only in payload (overwrite-in-place); the lexicon precedent uses stable IDs + deferred stale deletion — follow it.
required_receipt: Probe: rebuild templated_question_problem method twice (identical IDs), then delete that method entirely; counts for the other three methods and Mongo corpus_lexicon unchanged.

section: P2.2 Build Two-Sided Multi-Point Concept Representations
verbatim: Overfetch across representation methods, group by `canonical_key`, and score a concept by the strongest admissible representation (`max` with calibrated method policy), never by summing points or rewarding crowding.
action: Implement resolver-side grouping: overfetch representation points, group by canonical_key, score = max over admissible methods with a calibrated per-method policy table (versioned data, not Python constants); add a unit test where a 10-point concept with weaker best-match loses to a 1-point concept with stronger best-match.
where: backend/services/retriever/vocabulary.py (resolution stage); method policy as versioned config
why: Max-scoring is the structural guarantee behind acceptance L953 — crowding must be worthless by construction.
feasibility_note: Clean fit with the production-redesign direction (cross-encoder sole scoring authority, retire multi-score fusion — project_polymath_production_redesign): max-per-group is selection, not score fusion, so no conflict. Calibrated method policy must be versioned data per the no-hardcoded-rules invariant (checklist:62-63).
required_receipt: Asserting unit test (crowded-weak vs single-strong) green; live resolution trace on mark showing group-by-canonical_key with max method attribution in diagnostics.

section: P2.2 Build Two-Sided Multi-Point Concept Representations
verbatim: Prevent templated/generated/exploratory points from satisfying a required answer lane without original-query support or source-evidence descent.
action: Add a lane-satisfaction rule in the retriever: a required lane counts as covered only if backed by original-query support (protected_recall_lane) or completed source-evidence descent to parent/child chunks; templated/generated/exploratory point hits alone mark the lane nominated-not-satisfied; add a negative-control test.
where: backend/services/retriever/vocabulary.py + lane coverage accounting (evidence_lane_coverage side); future point resolver
why: This is the anti-drift wall: nomination by synthetic phrasing must never substitute for testimony by evidence.
feasibility_note: Terminology precision required (registry_retrieval): 'lane coverage' has TWO senses — recall_lane_coverage (selection) vs evidence_lane_coverage (evidence) — this rule binds the EVIDENCE sense; and the protected lane is protected_recall_lane (role=='original'). Enforces the standing invariant that expansion cannot replace the original query; overlaps acceptance L956 (same mechanism, different receipt).
required_receipt: Negative-control probe: a query answerable only via a templated point with no evidence descent yields lane=uncovered and honest refusal; trace artifact attached.

section: P2.2 Build Two-Sided Multi-Point Concept Representations
verbatim: Persist per-message query hash, selected corpus scope, resolved concept IDs, seated documents, answerability, evidence coverage, artifact epoch, and retrieval diagnostics in durable storage rather than stream-only telemetry.
action: Create a durable per-message retrieval-record collection (Mongo) written at answer finalization with the eight listed fields; hash the query (never raw text by default, anticipating L915/L918); make it the substrate the attested-loop reads.
where: backend/services (chat stream finalization); new Mongo collection (retrieval records); inputs from existing trace diagnostics
why: The attested-query loop and seat diagnostics are impossible over stream-only SSE telemetry that evaporates.
feasibility_note: All eight fields already EXIST transiently — gate+evidence-plan diagnostics ride the 'Local RAG retrieval' DONE trace metadata (live-chat-probe receipt) and query_hash machinery exists in eval_firewall.py/grounded_planner.py (probed today); the work is persistence, not derivation. Also anchors Phase 3's 'seat diagnostics' checklist item — one collection should serve both (avoid two telemetry stores).
required_receipt: After one authed live chat probe: a Mongo retrieval-record document exists with all eight fields populated and artifact epoch matching the current lexicon epoch.

section: P2.2 Build Two-Sided Multi-Point Concept Representations
verbatim: Harvest a user's phrasing as `attested_query` only when the answer passes answerability/evidence gates and the concept can be traced to selected final evidence; increment `resolver_hit_count` deterministically.
action: Implement the harvest gate over L909's durable records: attested_query is written only when answerability=true AND the concept traces to selected final evidence; resolver_hit_count increments exactly once per qualifying record (idempotent on record id, replay-safe).
who: backend service (attested-loop worker over retrieval records)
where: Mongo retrieval records -> corpus_lexicon (attested class, resolver_hit_count); backend answerability gate outputs
why: Gated harvesting is how real user language enters the lexicon without laundering failed or hallucinated answers.
feasibility_note: Answerability substrate exists and is well-mapped: registry_retrieval — chat gate = decision arbiter, retriever sufficiency = upstream repair signal; harvest must key off the CHAT-side gate (the arbiter), not retriever sufficiency. Deterministic increment closes the loop opened at L822. Blocked behind L909 and the L918 owner privacy decision (no harvesting before policy exists).
required_receipt: Replay test: processing the same retrieval record twice yields one attested_query row and +1 (not +2) resolver_hit_count; a failed-answerability record yields zero harvest.

section: P2.2 Build Two-Sided Multi-Point Concept Representations
verbatim: Store attested-query hash, timestamp, validation rule version, concept IDs, and evidence IDs without storing unnecessary sensitive conversation content.
action: Define the attested-record schema as hash+timestamp+validation_rule_version+concept IDs+evidence IDs; store the minimal concept-bearing phrase (needed for embedding) only under L918's adopted policy, never surrounding conversation; add a schema test that rejects raw-conversation fields.
where: corpus_lexicon attested entries / attested store; eval_firewall.py hash conventions
why: Minimization keeps the feedback loop useful for retrieval while keeping conversations out of durable indexes.
feasibility_note: Tension to resolve explicitly: a pure hash cannot be embedded — the attested REPRESENTATION point needs the phrase text, so 'hash-only' applies to the audit/firewall record while the phrase itself is governed by L918's minimization ('shorter concept-bearing phrase') — the two claims must be implemented as one schema to avoid contradiction. validation_rule_version mirrors the scorer_version pattern (registry_eval).
required_receipt: Schema + one harvested sample showing hash/timestamp/rule-version/IDs present, phrase field bounded (length cap), and no conversation-context field existing in the collection.

section: P2.2 Build Two-Sided Multi-Point Concept Representations
verbatim: Define explicit opt-in, minimization/redaction, retention, deletion, and corpus-isolation policy for attested phrases and their embeddings; do not promote personal narrative text when a shorter concept-bearing phrase is sufficient.
action: Draft the attested-data policy (opt-in default OFF, minimization rule, retention window, deletion path incl. embeddings, corpus isolation) as a one-page decision doc; obtain explicit owner sign-off BEFORE the first harvest runs; encode the adopted policy as config + deletion job, not prose.
where: decision doc in docs/; attested store + Qdrant attested points (deletion path); config
why: Personal phrasing entering durable vector indexes is a one-way door without a deletion and isolation policy.
feasibility_note: Genuine owner call — opt-in scope, retention, and deletion guarantees are policy, not engineering. Engineering substrate is ready-shaped: P0.6 deletion/orphan machinery (11 [x]) provides the deletion-pattern precedent, and corpus isolation matches the shared-collection multitenancy rules in the owner architecture. MUST land before L912 harvesting activates (sequencing gate inside S10).
required_receipt: Signed policy doc (date + owner) + an executed deletion drill: harvest one attested phrase on a test corpus, delete it, verify Mongo row and Qdrant point (and cache) are gone.

section: P2.2 Build Two-Sided Multi-Point Concept Representations
verbatim: Treat a failed query followed by a successful user rephrase as a pending alias/representation candidate; never auto-publish it without validation.
action: Detect fail->rephrase->success sequences over L909's durable records (session-scoped, time-bounded pairing rule), write them to a pending-candidates queue with both phrasings' hashes, and require validation (L912 gates + human/rule review) before any promotion to alias or representation point.
who: backend service (attested-loop worker) + owner/junior review of pending queue
where: Mongo retrieval records -> pending alias/representation candidate queue -> corpus_lexicon (on validation)
why: Fail-then-succeed pairs are the highest-signal lay-language aliases the system will ever see — but also the noisiest.
feasibility_note: Depends on L909 records including failed queries (answerability=false rows must also persist — confirm L909 schema covers refusals, not just successes). 'Never auto-publish' aligns with the S5 versioned alias registry as the publication target (registry_alias: curated + latent aliases fold there — pending candidates are a queue INTO it, not a fourth alias store).
required_receipt: Synthetic session test: fail->rephrase->success produces exactly one pending candidate and zero published aliases; publication requires an explicit validation action recorded with rule version.

section: P2.2 Build Two-Sided Multi-Point Concept Representations
verbatim: Reject every held-out/evaluator query hash from harvesting and verify the firewall before each evaluation run.
action: Wire eval_firewall.py into (a) the harvest path — reject any query whose hash matches the held-out/evaluator set at write time — and (b) an automated preflight asserting zero held-out hashes exist in attested/representation stores before every eval run (non-zero exit on hit).
where: backend/services/eval_firewall.py; attested store; run_heldout_eval harness entry point
why: One leaked eval question into the attested store silently converts every future A/B into a memorization test.
feasibility_note: This IS the 'automated contamination eval-run check' — registry_eval names it as the unbuilt half of eval_firewall, and PLAN_CRITIQUE S10 row schedules it 'with the first representation store'. The primitive (hash handling in eval_firewall.py) exists per code probe; the work is the two wiring points. Same receipt closes acceptance L957 and half of prerequisite L880 — implement once, cite thrice. After S10 it becomes a standing per-eval-run gate (CONT).
required_receipt: Green preflight log from a real eval run (0 held-out hashes) + red log from a synthetic contamination (planted hash) aborting the run with non-zero exit.

section: P2.2 Build Two-Sided Multi-Point Concept Representations
verbatim: Generate plain glosses, problem phrasings, goal phrasings, and example questions only from source-backed concept material after deterministic lift is measured.
action: HOLD until the S10 deterministic-method acceptance report (L948) exists; then, if lay-language recall is still short of target, build the generation lane: prompts consume only source-backed lexicon fields, outputs enter the generated provenance class, gated per L935.
who: backend service (Phase 4 generation lane; provider LLM)
where: P2.2 generation module (to-be-built); corpus_lexicon source fields in, generated-class points out
why: Paying LLM cost before measuring deterministic lift would make the lift unattributable and possibly unnecessary.
feasibility_note: Explicitly Phase-4-gated by the checklist ('Optional generated amplification (Phase 4 only)') and by Librarian Phase 4's own prerequisite ordering ('measure before LLM' is a Phase 3 box). The measured-lift precondition = L948's per-method report. Do not start alongside the deterministic S10 work — the gate is the point.
required_receipt: L948 acceptance report filed FIRST (the unblock receipt); then a generation-run manifest showing every prompt input traced to source-backed fields.

section: P2.2 Build Two-Sided Multi-Point Concept Representations
verbatim: Validate structured output and record provider, model, prompt, schema, and evidence versions.
action: When Phase 4 activates: enforce schema-validated structured output (reject-on-invalid, no repair-by-truncation) and stamp provider, model, prompt version, schema version, and evidence versions on every generated artifact; reuse the extraction contract's engine-identity pattern.
where: P2.2 generation module; generated-class lexicon material and points
why: Version stamps are what let a bad prompt or model generation be recalled and rebuilt without touching other classes.
feasibility_note: Pattern already exists: registry_extraction's 'extraction artifact contract + engine identity (schema_version + extractor + ExtractionContract)' — mirror it for the generation lane rather than inventing a second provenance mechanism. Mirrors the non-negotiable invariant (checklist:58-59, explicit model/engine/schema/evidence versions). Held behind the L929 gate.
required_receipt: One generated artifact showing all five version stamps + a validation test where malformed provider output is rejected with zero writes.

section: P2.2 Build Two-Sided Multi-Point Concept Representations
verbatim: Never create extractive fallback placeholders after provider failure.
action: Encode as a hard rule in the Phase 4 lane: provider failure = no write + counted failure (silent-fallback accounting), never a truncated/extractive placeholder; add a provider-failure test asserting zero rows written and the failure counter incremented.
where: P2.2 generation module; failure counters/diagnostics
why: The 67,953 empty-model summary placeholders were exactly this failure class — the system already paid for this lesson once.
feasibility_note: Standing invariant, not one-time work: mirrors the non-negotiable 'no empty-model or extractive fallback placeholder' (checklist:60-61) and the P0.1 cleanup receipts (0 empty-model summaries remain, PLAN_CRITIQUE sec.2 V; write-side guard precedent at qdrant_writer). Every fallback must count and surface its rate (silent-fallback-accounting rule).
required_receipt: Asserting test: simulated provider failure yields zero written artifacts + incremented surfaced failure counter; periodic probe shows zero generated-class rows with placeholder/empty provenance.

section: P2.2 Build Two-Sided Multi-Point Concept Representations
verbatim: Mark generated matches exploratory unless original user language or an admissible attested representation independently establishes the concept.
action: In the resolver, tag any concept hit whose ONLY support is generated-class points as exploratory=true (feeds L904's lane rule); clear the flag only when original-query support or an admissible attested point independently establishes the same canonical_key.
where: backend/services/retriever/vocabulary.py; representation point payloads (exploratory field from L896)
why: Exploratory tagging lets generated phrasing widen discovery without ever hardening into required evidence.
feasibility_note: Pure resolver logic over payload fields defined at L896; the 'independently establishes' test = same canonical_key matched via original lane or attested-class point in the same resolution. Held behind Phase 4 activation (no generated points exist until L929 unblocks), but the exploratory field and logic can ship with the deterministic pilot so the gate exists before the risk does.
required_receipt: Resolution trace: query matching only a generated point shows exploratory=true and no required-lane satisfaction; same query with attested support shows the flag cleared.

section: P2.2 Build Two-Sided Multi-Point Concept Representations
verbatim: A concept hit may contribute source-backed expansion terms, document nominations through `source_document_support`, and normalized concept IDs to librarian-card overlap; it cannot replace child/parent evidence.
action: Implement the three downstream contributions (expansion terms into the gated expansion lane; doc nominations via source_document_support; normalized concept IDs into card-overlap scoring) with the hard ceiling that concept hits never appear as final evidence; assert in the final-packet contract that all evidence rows are parent/child chunks.
where: backend/services/retriever/vocabulary.py; backend/services/librarian/shelf_engine.py; final selection packet contract
why: This is the operational form of 'vocabulary nominates; hydrated chunks still testify' — the layer's whole safety contract.
feasibility_note: Operationalizes decision L874 and the non-negotiable deterministic-seat invariant. Terminology: 'librarian-card overlap' keys on canonical_key/value_key (registry_alias); expansion contributions flow through the EXISTING grounded vocabulary expansion lane with the original-query lane protected (protected_recall_lane) — no new expansion mechanism.
required_receipt: Final-packet schema assertion in an e2e probe: evidence rows are exclusively parent/child chunk IDs; diagnostics show concept-hit contributions (expansion/nomination/overlap) recorded separately from evidence.

section: P2.2 Build Two-Sided Multi-Point Concept Representations
verbatim: Preserve corpus scope on every resolve point and globally merge cross-corpus results with corpus-local diagnostics and fair representation.
action: Carry corpus_id on every representation point (L896) and resolve per-corpus before merging; implement the cross-corpus merge with corpus-local diagnostics and route 'fair representation' through the existing P0.3 reservation_policy gate rather than a new floor.
where: backend/services/retriever/vocabulary.py; services/retriever/reservation_policy.py (P0.3 gate); representation point payloads
why: Cross-corpus concept resolution must not let a big corpus's point cloud drown a small corpus's only relevant concept.
feasibility_note: REUSE WARNING (registry_librarian): corpus_floor is already 'one idea, three implementations, two bounds' — 'fair representation' here MUST consume the calibrated reservation_policy (P0.3, merged and live-probed per PLAN_CRITIQUE sec.2) and not add a fourth implementation. Corpus-boundary preservation is a non-negotiable invariant (checklist:50) already tested in the cross-corpus P0.3 work.
required_receipt: Cross-corpus resolution trace (mark+ecom): per-corpus diagnostics present, merge honors the reservation_policy bound, and no resolve point lacks corpus_id.

section: P2.2 Build Two-Sided Multi-Point Concept Representations
verbatim: Report Recall@K, concept/document recall, nDCG/MRR, answer evidence coverage, storage, RAM, and p50/p95 for each representation method alone and cumulatively.
action: Run the held-out 56 per tier with method-ablation (each representation_method enabled alone, then cumulatively), producing an EVAL artifact with Recall@K, concept/doc recall, nDCG/MRR, evidence coverage, plus storage/RAM deltas and p50/p95 vs the 2026-07-13 baselines; preregister pass thresholds before the run.
where: run_heldout_eval harness / eval_metrics; docs/baselines EVAL_*.json artifacts; Qdrant storage stats
why: Per-method attribution is the only way to know which representation shapes earn their storage and latency.
feasibility_note: Harness exists (registry_eval: run_heldout_eval + eval_metrics, scorer_version-stamped artifacts; before/after gate's nearest seam = re-run per tier and diff EVAL_*.json). Method ablation requires representation_method payload filtering (L896) — cheap toggles. Latency context: recent deployed probes show Fast 39.8s degraded (checklist baseline) — capture a fresh post-S0 baseline first or the p50/p95 comparison is against a known-degraded number. Preregistered thresholds exist nowhere yet (registry_eval) — set them in advance.
required_receipt: EVAL artifact set: one per method + cumulative, each with all seven metric families, diffed against the refreshed baseline, thresholds declared in the artifact header.

section: P2.2 Build Two-Sided Multi-Point Concept Representations
verbatim: Lay-language Recall@K improves on held-out questions without direct expert query regression.
action: Partition the held-out suite into lay-language vs expert-phrasing rows (verify the held-out 56 actually contains enough lay rows; if not, extend the suite BEFORE any representation points exist so the firewall stays clean), then assert lay Recall@K improvement with expert Recall@K non-regression against preregistered margins.
where: held-out suite definition; run_heldout_eval; EVAL artifacts
why: Lay-language recall is P2.2's entire payoff claim; expert non-regression is the guard against gloss pollution.
feasibility_note: Risk is measurement power, not build: 56 questions split two ways gives thin cells — the suite-composition audit may force suite extension, which MUST precede point projection (any question written after points exist can leak into attested/generated material only if the firewall misses it; extending first keeps the ordering trivially clean). Terminology: 'the held-out 56' is the canonical suite name (registry_eval).
required_receipt: Suite-composition table (lay vs expert row counts) + EVAL diff showing lay Recall@K >= +preregistered margin and expert Recall@K within non-regression bound.

section: P2.2 Build Two-Sided Multi-Point Concept Representations
verbatim: Representation crowding cannot outrank a stronger single concept because one concept owns more points.
action: Verify by construction + probe: cite L901's max-scoring unit test (crowded-weak loses to single-strong) and run a live crowding probe on the mark pilot (pick the concept with the most points and confirm its rank equals its best single-point score's rank).
where: backend/services/retriever/vocabulary.py grouping logic; mark pilot Qdrant points
why: If crowding pays, every concept owner is incentivized to spam points and ranking integrity dies.
feasibility_note: Structurally guaranteed by L901's max-with-calibrated-policy scoring if implemented as specified — this acceptance is the verification twin of that build item. The only way it fails is a second scoring path bypassing the group-by (watch rank_fusion_fallback and qdrant_rrf paths, registry_retrieval fusion collision — assert they also group by canonical_key or run downstream of it).
required_receipt: Green L901 unit test + live probe artifact: max-point concept's final rank identical with N points vs best-1-point replay; all scoring paths audited for group-by coverage.

section: P2.2 Build Two-Sided Multi-Point Concept Representations
verbatim: Hub/junk concepts emit no query-facing points and gain no expansion rights.
action: Probe post-pilot: take the top-10 highest-DF/hub_status=true lexicon entries on mark; assert Qdrant contains zero representation points for them and a live vocabulary resolution grants them zero expansion terms while their evidence remains retrievable via chunks.
where: Qdrant representation points; backend/services/retriever/vocabulary.py expansion diagnostics; corpus_lexicon hub fields
why: Hub suppression at both emission and expansion is the concept-layer version of the facet DF lesson.
feasibility_note: Verification twin of L840 (fields+gates) and L883 (projector admission). Depends on the S5 hub_status substrate existing; if S7's facet owner decision adopts a DF threshold, keep the concept-side threshold independently calibrated (facet DF and concept DF are different distributions — registry_facet parent_df is the facet one).
required_receipt: Probe artifact: top-10 hub list with df numbers, 0 points each in Qdrant, resolution trace showing expansion denied, plus one chunk-level retrieval proving evidence retention.

section: P2.2 Build Two-Sided Multi-Point Concept Representations
verbatim: Generated or templated phrasing drift cannot misroute required evidence.
action: Run drift negative-controls: craft queries that match templated/generated points strongly but whose true evidence lives elsewhere (and forbidden-vocabulary controls); assert required lanes are satisfied only by original-query support or evidence descent (L904 rule) and final packets cite the correct chunks.
where: retriever lane-coverage logic; held-out negative-control rows; final selection packets
why: Drift misrouting is the failure mode that would convert P2.2 from a recall win into a citation-integrity loss.
feasibility_note: Acceptance twin of build item L904 — same mechanism, adversarial receipt. Negative-control machinery exists (registry_eval: negative_control heldout shape + 'forbidden-vocabulary control' — keep the two names distinct). Fail-closed behavior on absent answers is already a non-negotiable invariant (checklist:64), so the test pattern is established.
required_receipt: Negative-control run log: every drift probe either refuses honestly or cites correct evidence; zero cases where a templated/generated hit alone satisfied a required lane.

section: P2.2 Build Two-Sided Multi-Point Concept Representations
verbatim: Attested harvesting contains zero held-out query hashes.
action: Same receipt as L924: run the automated contamination preflight against the attested store and assert zero held-out/evaluator hashes; keep it wired to every eval run thereafter (standing gate).
where: backend/services/eval_firewall.py; attested store; eval harness entry point
why: A single leaked eval hash converts measured recall gains into memorization artifacts.
feasibility_note: Acceptance twin of L924 (build) and the second half of prerequisite L880 — one implementation closes all three; report them together to avoid triple-counting work. Becomes CONT (per-eval-run standing gate) after first green run.
required_receipt: Preflight artifact from the S10 acceptance eval: attested-store scan result '0 held-out hashes / N attested rows', signed with firewall rule version.

section: P2.2 Build Two-Sided Multi-Point Concept Representations
verbatim: Storage/latency increase remains inside the measured budget and the cache invalidates correctly after lexicon/tree/document epoch changes.
action: Declare the storage/latency budget numbers BEFORE the pilot (preregistered); after the pilot, diff storage/RAM/p50/p95 against them; then run the three-epoch invalidation drill: bump lexicon, tree, and document epochs in turn and assert the resolver cache misses-then-refreshes on each.
where: Qdrant storage stats; backend/services/retriever/vocabulary_cache.py (bump_corpus_epoch); latency probes vs docs/baselines
why: An unbudgeted representation store grows silently until it degrades the very latency P1.7/P1.9 bought back.
feasibility_note: Cache-epoch machinery exists at the lexicon seam (probed: bump_corpus_epoch wired from corpus_lexicon.py:3373-3391) but tree/document epoch triggers need verification — S0's live-verify is the prerequisite receipt. 'Measured budget' does not exist yet (registry_eval: preregistered thresholds appear nowhere) — setting it in advance is part of this item, per the hypothesis-promotion and silent-fallback-accounting rules.
required_receipt: Budget declaration artifact (pre-pilot) + post-pilot diff within budget + three-epoch invalidation drill log showing miss->refresh per epoch type.

section: P2.3 Add Query-Only Embedding Instructions
verbatim: Introduce a query-only embedding method distinct from document/concept/tree embedding.
action: Add an instruction-prefixed query embedding path in embedder.py (query lane only — embed_batch document/lexicon/tree callers untouched), off by default behind a config flag until L967's A/B passes.
where: backend/services/embedder.py (embed_queries path, _QueryEmbedBatcher :693); config for instruction toggle
why: Asymmetric query instructions are the cheapest known recall lever that touches zero stored vectors.
feasibility_note: Code probe today: embedder.py has ZERO instruction/prefix support (grep: no hits) but a clean query-side seam already exists (_QueryEmbedBatcher + query cache, distinct from document embed_batch) — the split this claim asks for is half-present structurally. SEQUENCING GAP: PLAN_CRITIQUE's S rows never slot P2.3 (S11 starts at P2.4) — nearest coherent window is alongside S10's query-side work after the S8 eval harness is warm; flag to the planner for an explicit slot before execution.
required_receipt: Diff showing instruction applied ONLY in the query path (document/lexicon/tree embed calls byte-identical inputs), plus a smoke probe: same query with flag on/off yields different vectors while a document re-embed yields identical ones.

section: P2.3 Add Query-Only Embedding Instructions
verbatim: Make instructions model-specific and versioned.
action: Store instructions as versioned config keyed by embedding model id (e.g. qwen3-embed -> instruction text + instruction_version), resolved at embed time; no Python conditionals per model (versioned data, not code — per the no-hardcoded-rules invariant).
where: backend/services/embedder.py; versioned config (model_id -> {instruction, version})
why: Instruction text that helps one embedding model can hurt another; versioning makes swaps and rollbacks auditable.
feasibility_note: Small config-plumbing task; the embedder already threads a config dict through the query path (embed cache keys hash config at embedder.py:816-825), so the carrier exists. Consistent with checklist invariant lines 62-63 (curated behavior belongs in versioned data, not Python conditionals).
required_receipt: Config artifact with model-keyed instruction + version; probe showing two different embedding model ids resolve different instructions and the version lands in diagnostics.

section: P2.3 Add Query-Only Embedding Instructions
verbatim: Keep raw query plus instruction version in the cache key.
action: Extend _query_cache_key(text, config) at embedder.py:836 to include instruction_version (raw query text is already in the key), and propagate instruction_version into the P1.7 resolver cache key (vocabulary.py:1697 keyed-by-knobs comment) so a toggled instruction cannot serve stale resolutions.
where: backend/services/embedder.py:816-881 (_query_cache_key, _QUERY_EMBED_CACHE); backend/services/retriever/vocabulary.py:1697 cache key
why: A cache blind to instruction version silently serves pre-instruction vectors and corrupts the A/B itself.
feasibility_note: Near-trivial given the probe: query text + config already hash into the key (embedder.py:816-836); the gap is instruction_version as an explicit key component in BOTH caches. Failure to touch the P1.7 resolver cache too would recreate exactly the stale-cache class S0's epoch work exists to kill.
required_receipt: Cache test: same query embeds twice with different instruction_version -> two cache entries, zero cross-hits; resolver cache probe showing a version flip forces a miss.

section: P2.3 Add Query-Only Embedding Instructions
verbatim: A/B test Qwen3 query instructions against the held-out suite.
action: Run the held-out 56 per tier twice (instruction off = the 2026-07-13-lineage baseline; instruction on) with preregistered promotion thresholds (Recall@K/nDCG margins + non-regression bounds) declared before the run; promote the flag only on a passing diff.
where: run_heldout_eval harness; EVAL_*.json artifacts; embedder instruction flag
why: The instruction is a hypothesis; only a held-out A/B with preset numbers can promote it (hypothesis-promotion rule).
feasibility_note: Harness and suite exist (P1.1 baseline [x]; before/after seam = re-run per tier and diff EVAL summaries, registry_eval). Confirm the deployed embed model is actually the Qwen3-instruction-compatible one on the MLX sidecar before writing instruction text — 'Qwen3' in the claim is a model assumption to re-verify, not a fact (check embedder config/model id first). Preregistered thresholds must be written down in advance (registry_eval: the phrase exists nowhere in the repo yet).
required_receipt: A/B artifact pair (off/on) diffed per tier with thresholds in the header and a promote/reject verdict; deployed model id recorded in both runs.

section: P2.3 Add Query-Only Embedding Instructions
verbatim: Do not re-embed documents unless the evaluation proves a document-side contract change is required.
action: Treat as a standing scope guard on the P2.3 experiment: document/tree/lexicon vectors are frozen for this work; any proposal to re-embed documents requires a failed query-only A/B plus an owner-approved document-side contract change with its own migration plan.
who: junior-agent (scope gate at review) / owner if a doc-side change is ever proposed
where: P2.3 experiment scope; Qdrant document/tree collections (untouched)
why: Query-side-only is what makes P2.3 cheap and reversible; a document re-embed is a paid migration with mixing risk.
feasibility_note: Aligns with the established embedding-source discipline: the runpod-embed promotion gate already rules 'never mix embedding sources within one collection' (checklist:815) — an instruction-changed document side would be a contract change of the same class. Cheap to enforce: L963's receipt (document embed inputs byte-identical) doubles as this guard's evidence.
required_receipt: Per-experiment attestation: zero document/tree collection writes during P2.3 (Qdrant collection update timestamps unchanged), cited in the A/B artifact.

section: P2.3 Add Query-Only Embedding Instructions
verbatim: Recall improves without corrupting document-vector compatibility.
action: Close via L967's A/B artifact (recall margin met) plus a compatibility check: assert document vectors are byte-unchanged (collection untouched) and instructed query vectors retain sane similarity distributions against a fixed document probe set (no wholesale score-scale shift that would break calibrated thresholds downstream).
where: EVAL artifacts; Qdrant document collections; similarity-distribution probe script
why: An instruction that lifts recall but shifts the score distribution silently breaks every calibrated gate downstream.
feasibility_note: 'Compatibility' needs the two-part operationalization above because documents are never re-embedded (L968) — corruption can only enter via query-side distribution shift affecting downstream calibrated consumers (reranker admission, answerability thresholds). The 0.98-cosine promotion-gate precedent (checklist:815) is the house pattern for this kind of compatibility number — preregister the analogous bound.
required_receipt: A/B recall diff >= preregistered margin + compatibility probe artifact: document collections unmodified and instructed-vs-uninstructed score-distribution shift within the preset bound.
